fact: May 12 is exactly 132 days into the year - that's almost exactly one-third of the way through!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby birds to hatch - their tiny peeps fill the air as they crack out of their shells!
Dandelions are going absolutely crazy right now, turning into those fluffy white puffballs that kids love to blow everywhere.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most northern places by this date, making mornings super bright and cheerful.
May 12 falls smack in the middle of Taurus season - the stubborn bull of the zodiac who loves food and naps.
In Australia and other southern places, leaves are turning brilliant orange and red as autumn kicks into high gear.
Earthworms are having a party in the soil right now because the ground is perfectly warm and moist for their slimy adventures.
This date sits exactly 40 days after the spring equinox in the north - nature's sweet spot for growth and greenness.
Fireflies start practicing their glow-up routines around this time, getting ready for their summer light shows.
The evening sky stays light until almost 8 PM in many northern cities, giving kids extra outdoor playtime after dinner.
Maple trees are dropping their helicopter seeds like crazy - those spinning propellers that twirl to the ground everywhere.
May 12 marks the time when morning dew gets really heavy, making grass sparkle like it's covered in tiny diamonds.
